title: Successful registration of a new user via signup form
preconditions:
Test Type: Positive.
User does not already have an account on automationexercise.com.
Browser is at https://automationexercise.com/login.

steps.expected: Login page is displayed with the 'New User Signup!' panel visible.

steps.content: Enter a unique Name and Email Address in the signup mini-form and click 'Signup'
steps.expected: Page navigates to /signup and displays the 'Enter Account Information' form.

steps.content: Fill in the required account information (title, password, DOB, name, address, etc.) and click 'Create Account'

steps.expected: User is redirected to the home page (/) and is automatically logged in — navbar shows 'Logged in as {Name}'.

siblingTitles: Registration with an already-used email is rejected
Login with an existing email but wrong password is rejected
Logout returns the navbar to guest state

title: Registration with an already-used email is rejected
preconditions:
Test Type: Negative.
Precondition: an account with a known email address already exists (e.g. provisioned via API createAccount).

steps.content: Enter a new Name and the EXISTING account's email in the signup mini-form, click 'Signup'
steps.expected: Inline error 'Email Address already exist!' is shown.
URL becomes /signup, NOT /login — known site behavior: the mini-signup form re-renders the same template at /signup on this error path instead of staying on /login.

siblingTitles: Successful registration of a new user via signup form
Login with an existing email but wrong password is rejected
Logout returns the navbar to guest state

title: Logout returns the navbar to guest state
preconditions:
Test Type: Positive.
Precondition: user is logged in with a valid account.

steps.expected: User is redirected to /login; 'Login' button is visible again; 'Logged in as' text is no longer present.

siblingTitles: Successful registration of a new user via signup form
Registration with an already-used email is rejected
Login with an existing email but wrong password is rejected

title: Logged-in user completes checkout end-to-end (Order Placed)
preconditions:
Test Type: Positive.
Known limitation: this environment has no payment sandbox — any card number is accepted with no Luhn/format validation. Do NOT create a "system rejects invalid card number" case against it; it will never fail.
Precondition: user has a valid account and is logged in.

steps.content: Open a product's detail page and click 'Add to cart', then 'View Cart' in the confirmation modal
steps.expected: Cart modal shows 'Your product has been added to cart.'; page navigates to /view_cart.
Page navigates to /checkout showing the order review and address summary.

steps.content: Fill in Name on Card, Card Number, CVC, Expiry Month/Year (any values are accepted — known limitation: no Luhn check on this environment) and submit payment
steps.expected: Page navigates to /payment_done/<order_id>; 'Order Placed!' heading is shown; 'Download Invoice' link is visible.

title: Payment rejects an invalid or malformed card number
preconditions:
Test Type: Negative.
Precondition: user has reached the /payment step with a valid cart.

steps.content: Enter a malformed card number (e.g. "abcd1234") in the Card Number field and click the pay button
steps.expected: An inline validation error is shown and the payment is rejected.

source: synthetic — violates known constraint #1 (payment.page.ts: no Luhn/format check, always accepted)

title: Login with a non-existent email shows a different error than a wrong password
preconditions:
Test Type: Negative.
Precondition: no account exists for the email used in step 1, and a separate valid account exists for step 2.

steps.content: Attempt login with an email that was never registered
steps.expected: Error message specifically says no account exists for this email.

steps.content: Attempt login with a registered email and a wrong password
steps.expected: Error message specifically says the password is incorrect (different text than the unknown-email case).

source: synthetic — violates known constraint #2 (identical message/response for both cases on the real site)

title: Checkout process works correctly
source: synthetic — passes the code grader's non-empty checks but is not clarity-checkable: no concrete text/URL/state to assert
